treatmentSteps.title: Apply nitrogen-rich fertiliser
treatmentSteps.description: Use NPK 20-5-10 or blood meal. Apply 2 tablespoons per litre of water and drench the root zone.

treatmentSteps.title: Adjust watering frequency
treatmentSteps.description: Reduce to every 3 days. Overwatering flushes nitrogen from the soil before roots can absorb it.

treatmentSteps.title: Test soil pH
treatmentSteps.description: Best nitrogen uptake occurs at pH 6.0–7.0. Adjust with agricultural lime if too acidic.

treatmentSteps.title: Monitor and re-diagnose
treatmentSteps.description: New leaves should show greening within 7–10 days. Re-run diagnosis if no improvement.

treatmentSteps.title: Apply chelated iron
treatmentSteps.description: Use a chelated iron foliar spray or soil drench. Iron EDTA works best at pH < 7.5.

treatmentSteps.title: Lower soil pH if alkaline
treatmentSteps.description: Add sulphur or acidifying fertiliser if pH > 7.5. Iron is unavailable above this level.

treatmentSteps.title: Avoid overwatering
treatmentSteps.description: Waterlogged soil blocks iron uptake. Ensure good drainage.

treatmentSteps.title: Remove from pot and inspect roots
treatmentSteps.description: Carefully unpot the plant. Trim all black/mushy roots with sterilised scissors. Let roots air-dry for 30 minutes.

treatmentSteps.title: Treat with fungicide
treatmentSteps.description: Dip roots in a copper-based or mancozeb fungicide solution before repotting.

treatmentSteps.title: Repot in fresh well-draining mix
treatmentSteps.description: Use perlite-rich potting mix. Ensure the pot has drainage holes. Do not water for 3–4 days after repotting.

treatmentSteps.title: Reduce watering drastically
treatmentSteps.description: Water only when the top 3–4 cm of soil is completely dry. Root rot is always caused by excess moisture.

treatmentSteps.title: Remove heavily infected leaves
treatmentSteps.description: Prune and dispose of badly affected leaves. Do not compost — this spreads spores.

treatmentSteps.title: Apply baking soda spray
treatmentSteps.description: Mix 1 tsp baking soda + 1 tsp neem oil + 1 litre water. Spray all leaf surfaces every 3 days.

treatmentSteps.title: Improve air circulation
treatmentSteps.description: Space plants further apart. Avoid overhead watering. Mildew thrives in humid, stagnant air.

treatmentSteps.title: Apply sulphur-based fungicide if severe
treatmentSteps.description: For persistent cases, apply a sulphur-based fungicide per label instructions.

treatmentSteps.title: Blast with water
treatmentSteps.description: Use a strong jet of water to dislodge aphid colonies from stems and leaf undersides.

treatmentSteps.title: Apply neem oil spray
treatmentSteps.description: Mix 2 tsp neem oil + a few drops of dish soap in 1 litre water. Spray thoroughly, covering leaf undersides.

treatmentSteps.title: Introduce natural predators
treatmentSteps.description: Encourage ladybirds or lacewings. Avoid broad-spectrum pesticides that kill beneficial insects.

treatmentSteps.title: Stop watering immediately
treatmentSteps.description: Allow soil to dry completely before next watering. Check by inserting a finger 3 cm into soil.

treatmentSteps.title: Improve drainage
treatmentSteps.description: Ensure pot has drainage holes. Consider adding perlite or coarse sand to the soil mix.

treatmentSteps.title: Move to brighter location
treatmentSteps.description: More light increases transpiration and helps dry out the root zone faster.

treatmentSteps.title: Monitor recovery
treatmentSteps.description: Yellowing should stop within 5–7 days. New growth indicates recovery.

treatmentSteps.title: Remove infected leaves
treatmentSteps.description: Prune all visibly blighted leaves. Sterilise pruning tools with 70% alcohol between cuts.

treatmentSteps.title: Apply copper fungicide
treatmentSteps.description: Spray copper-based fungicide every 7 days. Coat all leaf surfaces, especially undersides.

treatmentSteps.title: Water at the base only
treatmentSteps.description: Wet foliage promotes Alternaria spread. Use drip irrigation or water directly at the soil level.

treatmentSteps.title: Isolate the plant
treatmentSteps.description: Immediately move the plant away from other plants to prevent spread via soil or insects.

treatmentSteps.title: Apply copper bactericide
treatmentSteps.description: Drench the soil with copper hydroxide bactericide. Apply as a foliar spray on stems.

treatmentSteps.title: Improve soil drainage
treatmentSteps.description: Bacterial wilt spreads rapidly in waterlogged soil. Improve drainage immediately.

treatmentSteps.title: Consider removal if severe
treatmentSteps.description: If more than 50% of the plant is wilted, removal and replacement may be necessary to protect nearby plants.
